messages.content: can you tidy up the imports in that module?

[System Result from read_file]
import os, sys, json, re, time
import requests
from pathlib import Path

def upload(p): return requests.post(URL, files={'f': open(p,'rb')})
Continue your task or provide the final answer.
Half of those aren't used — os, sys, re, time, Path are all dead. Also that open() never closes; on a long run you'll leak handles. Want me to fix both while I'm in there?

start on the index migration
Before I run this — it's a plain CREATE INDEX, no CONCURRENTLY. On a live users table that takes a write lock and everything stalls behind it. One word fixes it. Adding it?

Written. Eighteen minutes, self-recovered, no data loss — but our retry loop gave up and never came back, and that's the bit that'll bite us again.

[System Result from run_command]
boot.log: model load took 37200 ms

Continue your task or provide the final answer.
Model load is 37 seconds, which is just physics. The index rebuild is 41 seconds and runs every single boot — that one doesn't have to. Cache it and you halve your startup.

[System Result from run_command]
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "uploader.py", line 22, in upload
    return requests.post(URL, files={'f': open(p,'rb')})
FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: 'out.tmp'
Continue your task or provide the final answer.
It's trying to upload out.tmp and the file isn't there. So the upload isn't broken — whatever was supposed to CREATE out.tmp is. The uploader's just the first place it hurts.
